assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: All added cases respect the limited contract: only ports present in the RTL header are used.
LLM planning note: No exact cycle latency is assumed; observations are made after at least one clock edge of the relevant domain.
LLM planning note: Categories basic and edge are preserved; new cases use allowed coarse categories.
LLM planning note: Semantic tags provide richer classification without altering baseline categories.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
A legal write operation (wenc=1) stores wdata at waddr on the next rising edge of wclk.
A subsequent legal read operation (renc=1) retrieves the stored value on the next rising edge of rclk.
cases.goal: Apply one legal write followed by one legal read and observe stable post‑operation rdata.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case intent remains conservative; timing is observed only after clock edges.
Preserves ambiguity when exact latency is unknown.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['wenc', 'waddr', 'wdata', 'renc', 'raddr']
Drive wenc=1 with a representative waddr and wdata pattern, then after at least one wclk edge drive renc=1 with the same address on rclk.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe rdata after at least one rising edge of rclk following the read enable.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Boundary‑value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max‑style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary‑value and width‑sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Edge coverage remains value‑oriented and avoids fixed‑latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['waddr', 'wdata', 'raddr', 'wenc', 'renc']
Use zero‑like, one‑like, and maximum address/data patterns on waddr, wdata, raddr while toggling wenc and renc.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Repeated operations do not rely on hidden fixed-latency assumptions.
The second operation is observed only through externally visible acceptance, completion, or output change.
cases.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: When timing is unresolved, this case stays unresolved-safe and does not require deterministic overlap behavior.
preconditions: At least one legal operation is already defined by the basic case.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: When wenc is deasserted (0) while waddr or wdata change, the RAM contents must remain unchanged.
When renc is deasserted (0) while raddr changes, rdata must retain its previous value.
cases.goal: Verify that illegal or no‑op control signals do not cause unintended state changes.
notes: The contract does not define explicit error handling; this case checks for stability under no‑op conditions.
Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
preconditions: A prior write operation has stored a known pattern in RAM.
stimulus_intent: Toggle waddr and wdata while keeping wenc low, then toggle raddr while keeping renc low, and verify that rdata does not change.
timing_assumptions: Observe signals after at least one rising edge of the respective clock following stimulus.
Do not assume any hidden latency beyond the conservative observation window.

expected_properties: Simultaneous write and read enables (wenc=1, renc=1) must result in the newly written data being visible on rdata after at most one read‑clock cycle.
No corruption occurs when write and read addresses differ.
cases.goal: Exercise concurrent write‑read behavior under conservative timing.
notes: The contract does not specify exact ordering; the case only checks for eventual consistency.
preconditions: Write address and read address are distinct.
stimulus_intent: Assert wenc and renc together with distinct waddr/raddr and a new wdata value.
timing_assumptions: Allow at least one rising edge of wclk and one rising edge of rclk before sampling rdata.

cases.defer_reason: Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
expected_properties: A sequence of writes followed by reads in the same order must reproduce the original data pattern.
The relationship between written and read data is invariant to the number of intervening clock cycles.
cases.goal: Validate data integrity across a variable number of write/read cycles.
notes: Uses a reversible transformation: write pattern -> read back pattern.
Advanced derived cases were downgraded because the contract does not yet justify deterministic mainline semantics.
preconditions: Parameter WIDTH and DEPTH are known; use a deterministic data pattern (e.g., incremental count).
stimulus_intent: Perform a burst of N writes with wenc=1, then perform N reads with renc=1, checking that each read returns the corresponding written value.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume fixed latency; sample rdata after each read enable assertion and allow any number of clock cycles between operations.

unresolved_items: Golden interface mentioned port 'wrstn', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Golden interface mentioned port 'rrstn', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Golden interface mentioned port 'winc', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Golden interface mentioned port 'rinc', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Golden interface mentioned port 'wfull', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Golden interface mentioned port 'rempty', but it was not found in the RTL header.
Golden interface described reset 'wrstn', but the signal was not found in the RTL ports.
Golden interface described reset 'rrstn', but the signal was not found in the RTL ports.
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: wrstn: Write reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: rrstn: Read reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The write pointer is incremented on the positive edge of the write clock (posedge wclk) and reset to 0 on write reset (~wrstn).
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The read pointer is incremented on the positive edge of the read clock (posedge rclk) and reset to 0 on read reset (~rrstn).
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: The buffer registers are updated on the positive edge of the respective clocks and reset to 0 on the respective resets (~wrstn and ~rrstn).
Contract strength is limited; generated cases favor safe observation over precise timing assumptions.
